facts.statement: Spouse A states they need acknowledgment before they can engage with logistics.
Both parties have stated a preference for an orderly process.
The emotional weight of the betrayal is present and acknowledged as process-relevant.
facts.note: Recorded as a process-relevant fact. Not a safety signal.

facts.statement: Spouse B has offered an acknowledgment of the harm caused.
facts.note: Accepted as a process-relevant acknowledgment. Not adjudicating the underlying relational claim.

facts.statement: Both parties want the children protected and both prefer an orderly process.
C9 boundary decision: emotional distress is present and acknowledged. The safety escalation threshold is not reached.
facts.note: Signals observed: emotional_distress_noted, pace_tension. No coercion signal, no incapacity signal, no acute safety indicator. M1 posture is appropriate. M2 is not triggered.

facts.statement: Spouse A agrees to begin discussing separation structure while reserving the right to return to the acknowledgment dimension.
Both parties identify minimising required joint decisions as the key structural preference.
Both parties accept the low-contact separation structure as a starting working basis.

facts_notes: Facts remain separated from unresolved feasibility questions.
